question: Can MagicBlock session keys (#[session_auth_or]/SessionToken) build against anchor-lang 0.32.1 + ephemeral-rollups-sdk 0.14.4 (anchor-compat), with NO anchor-1.0 migration?
verdict: GREEN, with a mandatory lockfile pin

rust_crate.name: session-keys
rust_crate.note: gpl-session 2.0.0 is the OLD gumhq lineage (anchor ^0.28 only); session-keys 3.1.1 is the current magicblock fork.

build_test.warnings: 13 (5 duplicates) — all cfg/unexpected-cfg noise, no errors
build_test.solana_program_note: two solana-program versions coexist (2.3.0 via anchor-0.32; 3.0.0 via ER SDK modern sub-crates). Tolerable — session-keys never crosses that boundary; identical shape to spike/live-er.
build_test.MANDATORY_PIN: Fresh resolve splits anchor-lang to 1.1.2 for session-keys (cargo picks highest in >=0.28,<2.0). Must force one node: 'cargo update anchor-lang@1.1.2 --precise 0.32.1'. A '=0.32.1' direct req alone does NOT collapse it.

client_npm.api: SessionTokenManager(wallet, connection) -> .program.methods.createSession(top_up, valid_until, lamports) / revokeSession; .get(sessionPda) reads the token
client_npm.note: @magicblock-labs/ephemeral-rollups-sdk 0.15.5 (npm) has NO session helper — delegation only. Session keys are a separate program (gpl_session).

privy_alternative.no_prompt_mechanism: useSignTransaction/useSendTransaction with options.uiOptions.showWalletUIs=false suppresses the confirmation modal for EMBEDDED wallets (React SDK).
privy_alternative.also_available: useSigners/addSigners + useHeadlessDelegatedActions + useSessionSigners (app authorization key as a signer; server/headless).
privy_alternative.program_change: NONE — the embedded wallet IS entry.player, already satisfies lock_pick's Signer/has_one auth.
privy_alternative.limitation: External wallets (Phantom) still pop per-tap; showWalletUIs only affects Privy embedded wallets.
